**Justification from established domain knowledge** (Cohen, Knowles et al., *PNAS* 2013; Linse/Knowles BRICHOS work, *Nat. Struct. Mol. Biol.* 2015): In the amyloid master equation, the aggregate mass fraction evolves with distinct microscopic rate constants for primary nucleation (kn), elongation (k+), and surface-catalyzed secondary nucleation (k2). Surface-active chaperone inhibitors like BRICHOS bind the fibril surface and *selectively* suppress secondary nucleation — the autocatalytic channel that dominates the sigmoidal burst and sets the half-time — while leaving primary nucleation (bulk) largely intact. This mechanistically supports dividing *only* the autocatalytic/elongation channel by the saturable inhibitor factor, exactly the selective-channel structure I am implement
